lens: Code reality & migration feasibility — verified against the actual god files (cmd/gc/session_reconciler.go, session_reconcile.go, internal/beads, internal/api) at HEAD e0eec587f.

blockers.issue: Rev-CAS ApplyStatus has no store support — one of the seven named error-killers is vaporware on the real Dolt/sqlite store.
blockers.why: internal/beads exposes no per-bead revision. currentDoltHash uses `PRAGMA data_version` (doltlite_read_store.go:424) which is GLOBAL — bumps on ANY write to ANY bead. The write path is SetMetadataBatch (caching_store_writes.go:384): last-writer-wins with a cache-diff idempotence guard, no expected-Rev param. Concrete failure: an external bd write to gc.status.* is pulled by the CachingStore reconcileLoop, then the reconciler's whole-Status write silently clobbers it — the promised 'heal loudly as drift' cannot fire because there is no Rev to detect the conflict (#1029/ga-ebb62d class returns, silently).
blockers.suggestedFix: Either build a real per-bead revision column in beads before claiming this killer, or downgrade it honestly to 'serial single-writer + idempotent convergence' and implement drift as an explicit read-back/compare/emit-event, not CAS.

blockers.issue: 'ApplySpec is the sole writer of gc.spec.*' is false: the API/CLI create session beads with metadata outside the reconciler.
blockers.why: internal/api/session_resolution.go:349 synchronously calls mgr.CreateAliasedNamedWithTransportAndMetadata(...extraMeta) — stamping identity/alias/template/extra metadata at user request, then Poke()s the reconciler and returns info.ID. Making this enqueue-only breaks the synchronous-create contract (caller expects the session to exist on return). So either single-owner is violated (reintroducing the exact 'path X stamped a subset of keys' shape the edge-triggered-stamping killer claims to eliminate) or interactive session creation becomes async — an uncosted UX/contract change that also collides with the in-flight worker-boundary 'single write site' migration (AGENTS.md; session_manager.go:14, session_resolution.go:349).
blockers.suggestedFix: Scope single-owner to reconciler-owned gc.status.* only; make gc.spec.* create-once-immutable shared between API-create and reconciler, or route all creation through the reconciler and accept async create explicitly in the migration cost.

blockers.issue: The differential-oracle safety net (Phase 2/3 'legacy trace stream IS its plan, soak to zero divergence before flip') is unsound because the legacy trace is incomplete by construction.
blockers.why: The god function (session_reconciler.go:1231-3420) has ~57 effect/mutation call sites but only 47 trace.RecordDecision calls, all behind `if trace != nil` and best-effort (trace_arms.go: //nolint:errcheck; collector can disable itself). Any mutation without a paired trace decision is invisible to the oracle, so 'zero divergence' can be a FALSE green: Decide could diverge on an untraced legacy effect and the oracle would never see it. The entire parity-pin safety argument rests on a trace that is not provably complete — and making it complete is more work on the very WAL the plan schedules for deletion.
blockers.suggestedFix: Make trace/plan completeness a hard Phase-2 gate: instrument the effect sites themselves (assert every mutation emits a plan step) or extract the legacy plan at the write boundary, not from the existing best-effort trace, before trusting any soak.

concerns: Cross-session Decide is a rewrite, not a 'widen.' The landed deriveConvergeActions (git c6b851ac1:cmd/gc/session_level_converge.go) is PER-SESSION (durableFacts, runtimeFacts for one bead, 246 LOC, 4 actions). The whole-fleet arms Windshield needs (min-floor spawn, sweepUndesiredPool, wake fairness) have NO landed pure counterpart and depend on 32 mid-tick `infoByID[id] = infoByID[id].ApplyPatch(...)` mutations that the min-floor scan reads this tick (session_reconciler.go:1508,1669,1843,1946,2192; tests literally named MinFloorCountReflectsMidTickClose*). 'Freeze the world once' directly fights code that requires mid-tick mutation visibility.

LOC/scope accounting is optimistic. cmd/gc has 3,019 `.Metadata[]` read accesses (not just the ~250 write sites), and there are 52 *Patch constructors (doc says 21). Confining the raw map to a codec package touches the read surface too; '~15K deleted / 4-5K added' looks light by a wide margin.

#3849 (resume-hard-fail crash-loop) is NOT killed today — it is landed but dormant. deriveFirstStart's crash-loop branch is 'inert until a real probe is passed (Stage 4)' and has zero non-test call sites passing a real transcript probe. The architecture stages this fix rather than delivering it.

One-probe-per-key consolidation is itself a behavior change: today workerSessionTargetRunning (1538) and workerObserveSessionTarget (1619) are separate probes with different flake exposure; collapsing to one bounded tri-state probe alters observation timing, so it can't be a pure no-op parity step.
gc trace's 185-code operator taxonomy and reconciler-debugging.md retraining (open Q #5) is real churn for on-call, though non-blocking.
mustFix: Resolve Rev-CAS feasibility BEFORE listing it as an error-killer: build per-bead revision in beads, or restate the hand-coherence-clobber protection as serial-writer + idempotent convergence with explicit read-back drift events (no silent clobber, but no CAS either).

Reconcile the multi-writer reality: state explicitly whether API/CLI session creation (session_resolution.go:349) becomes enqueue-only (async create, contract change) or remains a shared spec-create writer with single-owner scoped to gc.status.* — and sequence it against the existing worker-boundary migration so two 'single write site' efforts don't collide.

Gate the parity-pin on provable plan/trace completeness: instrument legacy effect sites to emit plan steps (57 effects must map 1:1 to steps) rather than trusting the best-effort `if trace != nil` stream; without this, 'zero divergence before flip' is not a safety guarantee.

Re-scope Phase 3 honestly: separate per-session arms (genuinely widen the landed deriveConvergeActions) from cross-session/whole-fleet arms (net-new pure functions replacing 32 mid-tick-mutation-dependent sites) — the latter are a rewrite and need their own parity strategy.
Correct the LOC/scope estimate to include the ~3,000 raw-metadata READ sites and 52 patch constructors, and mark #3849 as staged-not-fixed.
whatSurvives: The disease diagnosis is accurate, not a strawman — verified: the 2,189-line/god function spans 1231-3420, the providerAlive err→false collapse is real at 1538-1540, the 32 mid-tick mutations and isKnownState/isKnownStateInfo raw/Info twins exist verbatim. Two core primitives are genuinely sound and should NOT be second-guessed: (1) DeathCertificate/LiveTargetWitness making destructive Close/Teardown structurally uncompilable without a positively-confirmed probe — this kills the #2073 orphan-close class by construction, directly defusing the 1538-1540 collapse; (2) typed-total decode + sealed one-method-per-state handler killing unknown-state-skip-forever. IntentMarker two-phase is already proven working in the landed actionAttemptPrime/attemptEligible code. Level-triggered fixpoint and DST-as-proof are the right north stars. Session names are config-derivable (SessionNameFor), so pane↔bead adoption correlation works without stamped metadata.
verdict: Ship-with-changes. The north star and the witness/typed-decode primitives are correct and partly landed. But three load-bearing claims fail against real code: Rev-CAS has no store support, ApplySpec is not the sole spec writer (API creates beads), and the parity-pin's oracle trusts an incomplete best-effort trace. Fix those framings and the migration is credible; leave them and it's a disguised big-bang with two vaporware guarantees.

lens: Gas City invariants & principles (zero-roles, judgment-out-of-Go, layer purity, SDK self-sufficiency, S35, primitive-test, premature-abstraction)

blockers.issue: Rev-CAS is bundled into the shared 'mechanics kit' as a structural error-killer ('clobbering a newer write is structurally impossible') while Open Question #1 admits it may be infeasible on the Dolt/sqlite dual store — zero working implementations exist. This violates the project's 'no premature abstraction — don't build interfaces until two implementations exist' rule, and an error-killer that 'might not exist' is weaker than an honest runtime check.
blockers.why: If Rev is unavailable per-bead (the design's own open question), CASApply degrades to 'per-process serial exclusivity', the 'stale-snapshot clobber impossible' guarantee becomes false, and ApplyStatus ships an interface with no backing implementation — a speculative abstraction baked into a kit whose selling point is '2+ real users each'.
blockers.suggestedFix: Make ApplyStatus's proven contract (single serial writer + idempotent convergence) the baseline; ship CAS only after a real Rev source is proven, and do not phrase the clobber guarantee as 'structural' until it is.

concerns: Judgment re-entry via idleTracker fold-in: the DELETES list folds idleTracker into Decide/Quarantine, but idleTracker is 'if idle > timeout then act' — the exact time-threshold judgment #312 was reverted for and that nudgeStalledPoolClaims was rebuilt to AVOID by keying on bead state. A table-tested `now-lastActivity > cfg.IdleTimeout` arm is still a heuristic in Go; purity does not launder judgment. Decide's idle/sleep/wake arms must stay bead-state-keyed or escalate to prompts.

Durable quarantine (Open Q#2) reverses the documented in-memory OTP-restart stance AND removes a safety valve: a quarantined_until minted from a transient StoreErr/ProviderFlap now survives controller restart, wedging a healthy key forever — recreating the very 'stuck-forever' class OnUnrecognized was meant to kill. The existing circuit breaker deliberately ships a reset-generation knob; durable quarantine needs an equivalent first-class operator-reset effect.

Hard-rejecting raw writes to gc.spec./gc.status. (Open Q on bd contract) collides with an existing operator escape hatch: session_circuit_breaker's LogOpenOnce documents a 'manual reset knob', and operators hand-edit status to unstick sessions. The single-owner invariant is correct, but the operator-reset path must survive as a typed effect, not be silently removed with the raw SetMetadata ban.

Quarantine Guard's Admit→Quarantine is 'if key keeps failing then stop trying' — judgment-shaped. It survives ONLY because it mirrors the already-accepted mechanical rate-limiters (restartLoopGuard 3/60s, circuit breaker) and escalates recovery to prompts/humans rather than deciding strategy. Keep it strictly intensity/crash-count based; do not let it grow a 'why did it fail' branch.
mustFix: Unbundle Rev-CAS from the shared kit: baseline ApplyStatus = single-serial-writer + idempotent convergence (proven); CAS is an optional post-feasibility hardening, and the 'stale-clobber impossible' claim is downgraded from 'structural' until a real per-bead Rev exists.

Explicitly bar time-threshold idle/sleep judgment from Decide: idle-driven arms remain bead-state-keyed (per the #312 lesson and nudgeStalledPoolClaims precedent) or emit an escalation event to prompts — never `if idle>N then act` inside the pure core.

Add a first-class operator-reset effect (mirroring the circuit breaker's reset-generation) so durable quarantined_until and the gc.spec./gc.status. write ban do not eliminate the existing manual unstick path or create a durable spurious-quarantine wedge.

State that the Quarantine Guard is restricted to crash/restart-intensity counting with TTL+escalation, with recovery strategy left to prompts — it must not acquire any branch that reasons about the cause of failure.
whatSurvives: Zero-roles holds: verified no role-name conditionals in reconciler/desired-state; Snapshot/Decide/Effect are role-blind; DesiredWorld is config-derived. Layer purity is feasible: internal/ has zero cmd/gc imports today and build_desired_state.go imports only internal/ packages, so Observe/Decide can move down cycle-free. S35 is correctly honored (generic Engine rejected, per-domain state machines, kit = mechanics only). Witness types (DeathCertificate) are EARNED by the real providerAlive=false-on-error collapse and Commit by handler.commit — both pass the primitive test.
verdict: ship-with-changes. Windshield respects the invariants markedly better than the status quo — role-blind, config-activated, single-owner, witness-typed, SDK-self-sufficient — and its witness/Commit primitives are earned by real code. Three must-fixes: don't ship unproven CAS as a structural guarantee, keep idle/quarantine judgment mechanical (bead-state / intensity only), and preserve an operator-reset escape hatch.

lens: Distributed-systems soundness: crash-safety, delivery semantics, storage concurrency, observation fidelity, teardown ordering

blockers.issue: The DeathCertificate witness makes acting-on-*errored* observation uncompilable, but NOT acting-on-false-negative. Liveness comes from a batch `tmux list-sessions` cached in StateCache. 'Session absent from a SUCCESSFUL list' is indistinguishable from 'confirmed dead' — no error, so tri-state reports Dead, mints a valid DeathCertificate for a live session.
blockers.why: internal/runtime/liveness.go Liveness is {Running,Alive} bools (no Unknown). StateCache.staleTTL=30s returns Running=false for ALL sessions on fetch failure, and a mid-spawn or race-truncated list drops a live pane. Observed+Dead → DeathCertificate → teardown/close of a live session: exactly gc-hz0nu/#2073, unkilled.
blockers.suggestedFix: Freeze ONE batch list per pass; require corroboration (N consecutive absences or per-key HasSession + proctable cross-check) before Dead. Map staleTTL/list-error to Unknown. DeathCertificate demands corroborated-Dead, not single-list-absent.

blockers.issue: The 'clobbering a newer write is structurally impossible' error-killer via Rev-CAS ApplyStatus has NO storage backing. The beads store exposes no per-bead revision — the only CAS primitive is ReleaseIfCurrent(id, expectedAssignee), which compares the assignee field only. Rev-CAS is an unbuilt store feature stated as a fact.
blockers.why: grep confirms zero Revision/version-CAS in internal/beads. NativeDoltStore.SetMetadataBatch is a read-merge-write of the WHOLE metadata blob, so any concurrent writer (CachingStore reconcileLoop dirty-writeback, bd CLI, API, out-of-band `gc start/stop/restart`) lost-updates gc.status.* — the ga-ebb62d/#1029 clobber class survives.
blockers.suggestedFix: Either add a real per-bead revision to the store and gate ApplyStatus on it, or downgrade the claim to 'single-writer serial exclusivity' and enforce it — the controller flock does NOT cover CLI/API writers.

blockers.issue: 'Whole-value ApplyStatus written atomically' is a fiction across all three stores. The generalized commit() mechanic (handler.go:789) is a per-key non-transactional SetMetadata loop; a crash mid-struct tears the status. BdStore.SetMetadataBatch is documented 'not truly atomic for external stores.' No single marker gates a torn status struct.
blockers.why: commit() writes keys one-by-one then the marker; a crash after state=active but before instance_token leaves a half-status the next decode trusts. reconcile.go:509 even swallows the marker's error (`_ =`). Marker-last only saves idempotently-re-derivable writes, not a lifecycle-state struct.
blockers.suggestedFix: Route every ApplyStatus through one SetMetadataBatch AND gate the whole struct behind one trailing status-generation/hash marker; decode rejects any status whose marker != struct hash, so torn writes are ignored, not trusted.

blockers.issue: IntentMarker two-phase converts at-most-once loss into at-least-once redelivery — correct for idempotent effects, but StartSession (spawn) and AttemptPrime (nudge) are NOT idempotent. 'AtMostOneDuplicatePrimePerCrash' holds for ONE crash; N crashes in the confirm window yield N duplicate deliveries, and the confirm-marker write is itself non-atomic/swallowable.
blockers.why: attempted→nudge→confirmed: crash after nudge, before confirmed → Eligible() re-nudges (duplicate prompt to an LLM = non-idempotent). Start double-spawn is only half-guarded by ErrSessionExists, whose resolution (chat.go:382/494) leans on stale IsRunning=false — the same false-negative as blocker 1.
blockers.suggestedFix: Define a receiver-idempotency contract per non-idempotent effect: deterministic session name + provider ErrSessionExists for start (harden the IsRunning check); a delivered-token the agent dedups for prime, or explicitly accept duplicate prompts.

concerns: ConvergesWithinKTicks is a QUIESCENT-only invariant. Crash-looping sessions + pool min-floor keep the Plan non-empty forever by correct level-triggered design; it must not be sold as a production convergence bound — only as a test property under a frozen environment.

Snapshot is not actually frozen for liveness: StateCache has a 2s TTL and refreshes lazily per-access, so a long observe pass over a large fleet can straddle a refresh — key A observed at cache-gen N, key Z at N+1. Whole-fleet Decide's pool-floor count is then computed over a torn liveness view and can oscillate fleet size. Requires one up-front frozen batch list.

OnUnrecognized → Quarantine{TTL}+event REVERSES the current deliberate forward-compat behavior (session_reconciler.go:~1522 skips unknown states so an OLD controller tolerates a NEWER controller's new state strings during rolling deploy). New design would have the old controller quarantine live beads a new controller legitimately manages. Gate quarantine on same-version, or keep ignore-with-telemetry.

The controller flock (.gc/controller.lock) enforces single-controller only per HOST and only steady-state. In the hosted multi-replica Dolt topology this fork targets, two hosts each take their own flock and both write. Plus every out-of-band `gc start/stop/restart/handoff` and the API writes gc.status/spec WITHOUT the lock — so 'single writer per bead' is already false today.

Teardown double-free: proctable.KillByPID(r.PID) on a witness that is 'valid one tick' can still hit a REUSED PID under a busy fleet if teardown lands a tick late, killing an unrelated process. PID-based termination needs a start-time/cmdline recheck at the kill site, not just witness freshness.
mustFix: Make tri-state liveness real at the SOURCE, not just in the Decide type. Take one frozen batch list per pass; a config-known key absent from a SUCCESSFUL list is Unknown-until-corroborated (consecutive-absence count or proctable cross-check), and only corroborated-Dead may mint a DeathCertificate. StateCache staleTTL/fetch-error must yield Unknown, never Running=false. Without this, blocker-1's orphan-close class is fully intact behind the new witness types.

Downgrade the CAS error-killer to its true strength in the doc: 'single-writer serial exclusivity + idempotent convergence,' NOT 'clobber structurally impossible' — unless a per-bead revision is actually added to the beads store. Enumerate the real concurrent writers (caching reconcile writeback, bd CLI, API handlers, out-of-band gc subcommands, multi-host replicas) and state how each is fenced or healed-loudly.

Every ApplyStatus must be one atomic SetMetadataBatch gated by a single trailing status-generation marker (hash of the struct); decode ignores any status whose marker mismatches. This closes torn-write partial-status AND gives the missing dedup gate the whole-value framing omitted.

Establish an explicit idempotency/dedup contract for each non-idempotent effect (start, prime). Do not present at-least-once redelivery as solved by the two-phase marker alone; the marker bounds retries, the RECEIVER must dedup. Name the token and the enforcing layer for each effect.

Reframe ConvergesWithinKTicks as quiescent-only and preserve rollback forward-compat: OnUnrecognized should ignore-with-TTL-telemetry (or same-version-gated quarantine), not unconditionally quarantine, so a mixed-version rolling deploy cannot quarantine live sessions.
whatSurvives: The Snapshot→Decide→Apply level-triggered spine is sound and is the right destination — it is what internal/convergence/reconcile.go already does well and what K8s controllers do; boot=first-tick is correct. Marker-last-as-a-trailing-parameter (handler.go commit) is a genuine, already-working 'unrepresentable-to-violate' construction. Pure/total Decide + determinism-lint + table tests + simworld DST is a strong, achievable testability investment. The two-phase priming analysis (stamp-after-nudge) is correct. Witness types DO kill the err→false collapse class, even if not the false-negative class.
verdict: Ship-with-changes, but reword two marquee error-killers. The spine and testability are sound. However 'witness makes orphan-close uncompilable' and 'CAS makes clobber structurally impossible' do NOT hold against the real batch-list liveness (no Unknown for false-negatives) and the real store (no revision, whole-blob read-merge-write, uncovered out-of-band writers). Downgrade both to honest single-writer + corroborated-observation guarantees, or build the substrate first.

lens: YAGNI / is-this-actually-simpler: the pragmatist's knife. Would a junior debug this faster at 3am, or is it relocated complexity with new vocabulary?

blockers.issue: The simworld DST 'proof system' is the load-bearing testability claim, but it is entirely aspirational AND structurally blind to where the confirmed bugs live. Zero property/DST tests exist today (grep for testing/quick, rapid, gopter, Injector, seeded sim = nothing). The fake runtime provider (internal/runtime/fake.go) does NOT execute real sessions — the project's own memory notes 'fake≠executes→subprocess tier'.
blockers.why: The two highest-value bugs this redesign exists to kill — the providerAlive err→false collapse (session_reconciler.go:1538-1540, confirmed) and pane leaks — live at the Layer-0 tmux/subprocess boundary that the fake stubs out. A green simworld soak over memstore+fake proves Decide converges over a MODEL, not that real reconciliation is safe against a tmux blip. You'd ship 'military-grade' confidence that never touched the seam that caused the incidents. Building+maintaining a nightly seeded DST + tmux conformance suite is a multi-quarter effort with its own flake budget (an open question in the doc), competing with the god function it's meant to retire.
blockers.suggestedFix: Demote DST to a stretch goal. Ship the one testability layer that's real and already landed — table tests over the pure per-session core, one row per historical bug. Add the tmux conformance suite ONLY for the witness/probe boundary. Do not gate the redesign on a whole-fleet simulator that stubs Layer 0.

blockers.issue: The migration's continuity story — 'widen the landed deriveConvergeActions into Decide' — is a category error. It is a from-scratch rewrite disguised as an incremental graft, so the Phase-3 safety net ('each arm soaks to zero divergence, delete legacy arm on flip') rests on a false premise.
blockers.why: The landed core is per-session `deriveConvergeActions(d durableFacts, r runtimeFacts) []sessConvergeAction` — 4 enum actions, ~30 LOC of logic (session_level_converge.go:214-246). The proposed Decide is whole-fleet `Decide(Snapshot) Plan` with a 6-method sealed interface, an 11-variant proof-typed Effect sum, and witnesses threaded through every destructive effect. Different signature, different arity (per-key→fleet), different type discipline. You cannot 'widen' one into the other arm-by-arm; you rewrite the harness first, at which point the parity pin is comparing a new engine to the old, not extending a shared one. The doc's own safety claim evaporates.
blockers.suggestedFix: Keep the per-session signature that already landed and grow it arm-by-arm behind the S19 parity pin. Drop whole-fleet Decide, the sealed handler graph, and the Plan/Effect sum from the critical path; revisit only if a per-session enum demonstrably cannot express pool min-floor / wake fairness.

blockers.issue: The Mechanics kit (Commit/IntentMarker/CAS), the Applier/SSA-lite write layer, and Rev-CAS are scheduled to be BUILT in Phase 0–4 but their justifying second/third consumers don't arrive until Phase 7 — a direct violation of the project's settled rule 'no premature abstraction: don't build interfaces until two implementations exist.' Rev-CAS additionally rests on an unconfirmed primitive.
blockers.why: IntentMarker has exactly ONE real user today (priming, session_level_converge.go). Dispatch's sentinels (ErrControlPending/ErrControlGraphMalformed) are used ~10+ times and only migrate to sealed Outcome in Phase 7 — behind an errors.Is-compat shim that keeps the sentinels alive anyway, so the 'deletion' is cosmetic. The doc's own OPEN QUESTION #1 admits it doesn't know whether Dolt/sqlite exposes a per-bead revision for CAS; if it doesn't, ApplyStatus 'degrades to idempotent convergence' — i.e., you rebuild 1855 SetMetadata call sites onto a two-applier write layer to buy a guarantee that may not exist. That's maximal relocation of complexity for contingent payoff.
blockers.suggestedFix: Cut the shared kit and SSA-lite Applier from the plan. Prove Rev-CAS against the real store FIRST as a spike; only if it holds, and only after a second domain genuinely needs Commit/IntentMarker, extract the kit. Until then keep write-side fixes local to the session codec.

concerns: The 6-method sealed handler (OnAbsent/OnCreating/OnActive/OnDraining/OnQuarantined/OnUnrecognized) under-models the state space actually in the code: grep finds 13 live state strings (active 450×, creating 219×, idle 105×, pending, orphan, draining, ghost, quarantined, sleeping, dead, terminated, waiting, starting). Either the collapse to 6 is real — and all today's idle/sleeping/pool/ghost logic relocates into OnActive/OnUnrecognized, so the god function's complexity moves rather than shrinks — or the interface grows back toward 13 methods. Compile-forced totality over the wrong partition doesn't reduce what you hold in your head.

The flight recorder (good idea, ~300 LOC replacing the 5,124-LOC WAL) and the Phase-2/3 differential oracle both depend on the trace WAL as a faithful plan projection — but the WAL is deleted in Phase 6. You must keep the very subsystem the redesign calls a 'symptom' fully alive and correct through five migration phases.

Whole-fleet serial Decide is O(fleet) decode+decide every 30s tick with 'deliberately no worker pool.' Fine now, but it bakes a serial ceiling into the core and is flagged as an open question — a scaling decision made implicitly by an architecture choice, not by need.

Net-LOC framing ('~15K deleted, 4-5K added') undersells that the ~4-5K added (simworld, flight recorder, Applier, kit, Guard, witness plumbing) becomes the NEW hardest-to-hold code. The bet that the new core is easier to debug at 3am is only clearly won for the pieces already landed.
mustFix: Reduce the shipped scope to the three primitives that map 1:1 to the bug corpus and are already in flight: (1) witness types on destructive effects, (2) two-phase IntentMarker priming (LANDED), (3) typed session state with an OnUnrecognized→quarantine escape hatch replacing raw-string skip-forever. Everything else is deferred, not scheduled.

Keep the EXISTING per-session pure-core signature (deriveConvergeActions) and grow it arm-by-arm behind the S19 parity pin. Remove whole-fleet Decide, the sealed handler graph, and the Plan/Effect closed sum from the critical path — reintroduce only when a per-session enum provably cannot express a concrete cross-session need.

Drop the Mechanics kit, SSA-lite Applier, and Rev-CAS from Phases 0–4. Gate any of them on (a) a proven Rev primitive against the real Dolt/sqlite store and (b) a second real consumer existing first, per the project's own two-implementations rule.

Reframe DST/simworld as a research spike, not a merge gate. The redesign must be shippable and safe with table tests + a tmux conformance suite at the witness boundary alone. Do not let a whole-fleet simulator that stubs Layer 0 become a dependency of the safety story.

State explicitly how the 13 observed states map onto the 6 handlers before committing to the sealed interface, so reviewers can see whether complexity is removed or relocated into OnActive/OnUnrecognized.
whatSurvives: Four things are genuinely sound and should NOT be second-guessed: (1) the witness types (DeathCertificate/LiveTargetWitness) — the providerAlive err→false collapse at session_reconciler.go:1538-1540 is real and authorizes teardown of live sessions; making the destructive effect uncompilable without a positive probe is the single highest-value, lowest-cost idea here. (2) Two-phase IntentMarker priming — already landed, a correct at-most-once/at-least-once fix. (3) The observe→decide→apply pure core with a clock-free table-testable diff — already landed dormant, the proven direction. (4) Typed state replacing raw-string skip-forever.
verdict: ship-with-changes. The correct core (~3 primitives) is already landing incrementally as S19; 'Windshield' rebrands that and bolts on ~7 speculative primitives (whole-fleet Decide, sealed handler graph, Effect/Outcome sums, SSA-lite Applier, shared kit, Quarantine Guard, simworld, flight recorder) whose consumers/foundations don't exist yet. Cut ~60% of it, keep growing the landed per-session core, defer everything without a second consumer or a proven Rev primitive.
